result.summary: Atlassian exposes Cloud HTTP APIs (e.g. Jira REST), a platform GraphQL API, and authenticates mainly with Atlassian OAuth bearer tokens or Atlassian API-token Basic auth, plus admin APIs with Admin API keys and some product-specific credentials like Bitbucket tokens/app passwords and Trello key+token.

atlassian_api_token.label: Atlassian account API token
atlassian_api_token.setup: Create an API token for your Atlassian account from [Manage API tokens for your Atlassian account](https://support.atlassian.com/atlassian-account/docs/manage-api-tokens-for-your-atlassian-account/). Use it together with your Atlassian account email for Basic auth against site-hosted REST/GraphQL endpoints, or base64-encode `email:api_token` for Atlassian Rovo MCP Basic auth as shown in [Configuring authentication via API token](https://developer.atlassian.com/cloud/rovo-mcp/guides/configuring-authentication-via-api-token/).

atlassian_oauth_app.label: Atlassian OAuth 2.0 app access token (3LO / OAuth 2.1 where documented)
atlassian_oauth_app.setup: Create an app in the [Atlassian developer console](https://developer.atlassian.com/console/myapps) and configure OAuth for your integration. For Cloud product REST APIs, use the OAuth 2.0 authorization code grant (3LO) as referenced in the Jira and Confluence auth docs. For TWG CLI and Rovo MCP interactive flows, follow the product docs to authenticate with Atlassian OAuth in-browser (for example `twg login` or your MCP client's OAuth flow) using [Configure OAuth 2.1 for Rovo MCP](https://developer.atlassian.com/cloud/rovo-mcp/guides/configuring-oauth-2-1/) and [TWG CLI authentication](https://developer.atlassian.com/cloud/twg-cli/getting-started/how-authentication-works/).

admin_api_key.label: Atlassian Admin API key
admin_api_key.setup: As an organization admin, create an Admin API key from Atlassian Administration by following [Manage an organization with the admin APIs](https://support.atlassian.com/organization-administration/docs/manage-an-organization-with-the-admin-apis/). Copy the API key and your organization ID when shown; the docs note the key is shown only once. Use it as `Authorization: Bearer <api_key>` with Cloud Admin APIs, and with `twg admin auth login --email <your-email> --org <your-org-id>` for TWG CLI admin authentication.

bitbucket_access_token.label: Bitbucket access token (repository, project, or workspace)
bitbucket_access_token.setup: Create a Bitbucket repository, project, or workspace access token from the relevant Bitbucket Cloud admin UI as described under [Bitbucket REST API authentication methods](https://developer.atlassian.com/cloud/bitbucket/rest/intro/#authentication). Choose scopes for the resources you need. Use the token as a Bearer token for Bitbucket Cloud REST API calls; TWG CLI also prompts for a separate Bitbucket token because Bitbucket commands are not covered by Atlassian OAuth.

bitbucket_app_password.label: Bitbucket app password
bitbucket_app_password.setup: Create an app password for your Bitbucket account from Bitbucket Cloud as documented in [Bitbucket REST API authentication methods](https://developer.atlassian.com/cloud/bitbucket/rest/intro/#authentication). Grant the needed scopes, then use it with your Bitbucket username for Basic auth to the Bitbucket REST API.

trello_api_key_token.label: Trello API key and user token
trello_api_key_token.setup: Create a Trello API key for your Power-Up/application and generate a user token following Trello's authorization docs at [Trello REST API authorization](https://developer.atlassian.com/cloud/trello/guides/rest-api/authorization/). Trello REST requests commonly send both `key` and `token` together as query parameters.

directory_api_key.label: Atlassian directory API key for SCIM user provisioning
directory_api_key.setup: In [admin.atlassian.com](https://admin.atlassian.com/), open your organization, go to **Security** → **Identity providers**, choose the directory, then regenerate the directory API key as described in the [User provisioning REST API authentication docs](https://developer.atlassian.com/cloud/admin/user-provisioning/rest/intro/). Copy both the directory base URL and API key when shown; the key is used as `Authorization: Bearer <api_key>` for SCIM directory endpoints.

service_account_api_key.label: Atlassian service account API key
service_account_api_key.setup: Create a service account API key using Atlassian's API Access / service account administration flow referenced by the [API Access REST API](https://developer.atlassian.com/cloud/admin/api-access/rest/intro/) and [Rovo MCP API-token auth guide](https://developer.atlassian.com/cloud/rovo-mcp/guides/configuring-authentication-via-api-token/). Use it as `Authorization: Bearer <api_key>` for supported service-account and MCP scenarios.
